free_text: Measurement of the 65Cu(n,gamma) cross section using
the Detector for Advanced Neutron Capture Experiments
at LANL
Experiment performed at the Lujan
Neutron Scattering Center at LANSCE
Metallic cylinder with diameter
of 4 mm and total mass of (219.2+-0.5) mg.
For gamma ray detection detector DANCE was
used. It is an array consisting of 160 BaF2 detectors
with solid angle coverage of almost 4 Pi.
Time of flight was used to determine incident
neutron energy. Proton beam was pulsed with frequency
of 20 Hz and the DANCE detector was located at flight
path length of 20.28 m.
Normalization was performed over the 4.89-eV resonance
- below 20 keV
- above 3 keV
Spallation reaction of 800 MeV protons from
proton linac incident on a pair of tungsten targets
and subsequently moderated by room-temperature
backscatter water moderators.
Compiled by S.H.
Data taken from fig. 8 of the reference.
Waiting for data from author
The overall systematic uncertainty in neutron fluence
normalization was 7.8%, consisting of components
given below as ERR-1 through ERR-4.
Syst. uncertainty in number of 197Au atoms
Syst. uncertainty in 197Au Esum shape
Syst. uncertainty in normalization factor
Syst. uncertainty in 197Au cross section
Syst. uncertainty in number of 65Cu atoms
Syst. uncert. in DANCE 65Cu cascade efficiency
Total systematic uncertainty
Errors shown in Fig.8
Data taken from tab. 3 of the reference
Statistical uncertainty
Systematic uncertainty
